question: Track exactly why the Wave 1-11 and current build-prep index refreshes happened, especially why targeted validators were not always active-indexed immediately.

summary.core_logic: Implementation waves were proven by owning validators and focused tests. Broad repo/script indexes were refreshed only when a later discovery/reconciliation/closeout step needed them.
summary.main_finding: Wave 1-7 validators were intentionally not globally routed or active-indexed immediately. Wave 8 owned helper/script/index reconciliation, so Wave 1-7 validators first became indexed tracked files there. They became active dispatch surfaces later when Wave 11 updated configs/v2/script_surface_usage_policy.json and the post-Wave-11 closeout refresh rebuilt the indexes.
summary.current_state: The current durable protocol now makes this explicit: new wave validators are direct owner-proof surfaces first; refresh repo/script indexes only when future discovery or another explicit refresh condition requires it.

mechanics.generated_index_commit_model: Generated refresh commits index their parent implementation head. The script-import summary head and repo-index receipt git_head_before/git_head_after point to the parent commit, not the generated-output commit itself.
mechanics.why_head_only_staleness_appears: After a generated-output commit is created, the committed generated artifacts necessarily describe the parent head. That can create head-only staleness if treated mechanically. The current protocol classifies that as evidence and says not to chase it unless a real refresh condition applies.
mechanics.script_import_active_meaning: A script can be indexed as a tracked file but still have active_allowlist_member=0 and dispatch_allowed=0 until configs/v2/script_surface_usage_policy.json routes it as keep/active. The index reflects the policy state of the captured head.

refresh_events.subject: Reconcile Wave 8 helper and index discovery
refresh_events.why_refresh_happened: Wave 8 explicitly owned helper manifest, script-surface, repo-index, script-index, config-inventory, and generated discovery reconciliation after Waves 1-7 had added new owner surfaces.
plan_evidence: plans/v7_2_4/vbtprobuild_crack_repair_execution_plan_20260513.json#/execution_order/8/role = Align helper catalogs, repo indexes, script indexes, and generated discovery truth with current filesystem truth.

execution_order.8.refresh_sequence says to run Wave 1-7 focused validators, refresh script import architecture discovery, refresh full repo-index after Wave 1-7 surfaces are committed, refresh config inventory, and commit generated refresh artifacts only as Wave 8 reconciliation output.

execution_order.8.nonblocking_before_wave_8 says repo-index stale status, new Wave 1-7 tracked files missing from the old repo-index snapshot, and script import architecture freshness drift are nonblocking before Wave 8.
refresh_events.validator_indexing_effect: Wave 1-7 validator files existed in the script index as tracked scripts, but they were not yet active_allowlist_member=1 or dispatch_allowed=1 because script_surface_usage_policy had not yet globally routed them.

refresh_events.subject: Refresh Wave 10 discovery indexes
refresh_events.captured_head_subject: Add Wave 9 MCP runtime precedence gate
refresh_events.why_refresh_happened: Wave 9 added a new MCP/runtime validator and policy surfaces. The next plan action was Wave 10 read-only/index mutation cleanup, which needed to discover and audit the Wave 9 validator for no-write behavior.
plan_evidence: At captured head a2c31d7c, next_action says: Prepare Wave 10; include the Wave 9 MCP/runtime precedence validator in summary-only, repo-corpus, and pytest no-write audits.
execution_order.9.future_wave_details says Wave 10 should audit scripts/v2_validate_mcp_runtime_precedence.py for accidental writes during summary-only, repo-corpus, and pytest flows.
execution_order.8.future_wave_details says Wave 10 should use the refreshed index to find validators or tests that still mutate receipts, checkpoints, indexes, or generated summaries.
refresh_events.validator_indexing_effect: The Wave 9 validator became discoverable as an indexed tracked script, but it was not active allowlisted yet because the global script policy had not added it.

refresh_events.subject: Refresh Wave 11 discovery indexes
refresh_events.why_refresh_happened: Wave 10 added the reusable read-only command harness and routed it in script_surface_usage_policy. Wave 11 scanner/exact-path cleanup needed the fresh harness and discovery baseline.
plan_evidence: At captured head e1d27aba, next_action says: Prepare Wave 11; use the Wave 10 read-only command harness to distinguish stale scanner/index evidence from actual helper mutation defects.

execution_order.10.future_wave_details says Wave 11 should use Wave 10 read-only results to distinguish stale scanner evidence from actual mutating helper defects and prefer scripts/v2_validate_read_only_command_harness.py over one-off git status checks.
refresh_events.validator_indexing_effect: The active allowlist count rose by one because the Wave 10 read-only harness was globally routed before this refresh.

refresh_events.subject: Refresh indexes for post Wave 11 closeout
refresh_events.why_refresh_happened: The closeout checklist explicitly required repo/script/config index refreshes, then focused Waves 1-11 validation and post-refresh status checks. This was the decisive refresh that rebuilt script-import indexes after Wave 11 had globally routed the Wave 1-11 validators.
plan_evidence: At captured head 9dbd6a35, next_action says to execute post_wave_11_closeout.execution_checklist: snapshot status, refresh repo/script/config indexes, run Waves 1-11 focused validators and read-only harness, run status/cleanliness/diff checks, then commit generated refresh outputs if clean.
post_wave_11_closeout.execution_checklist.refresh_commands names output_schema_and_full_repo_index, script_import_architecture_index, and config_variable_inventory refresh commands.

post_wave_11_closeout.pass_fail_rules classify repo-index, script-import, exact-path, scanner, handoff, or superseded-plan evidence-only findings as warnings unless paired with a required validator/test/safety failure.
script_policy_evidence: Commit 413b07fc added scripts/v2_validate_scanner_exact_path_legacy_authority.py and routed Wave 1, Wave 3, Wave 4, Wave 5, Wave 6, Wave 7, and Wave 9 validators in configs/v2/script_surface_usage_policy.json.
The script-import active_allowlist_files_represented count jumped from 159 to 215 after this policy expansion was captured.
refresh_events.validator_indexing_effect: All Wave 1-11 validators became active allowlist and dispatch surfaces here because the captured head included the Wave 11 script_surface_usage_policy expansion.

refresh_events.subject: Refresh indexes after closeout commit
refresh_events.why_refresh_happened: The closeout commit added checkpoint and receipt artifacts and marked the crack-repair plan complete. This final refresh indexed those closeout artifacts and confirmed the Wave 1-11 validator routing stayed active.
plan_evidence: The closeout plan said that after committing refresh outputs, status checks should be rerun and the plan marked complete only if no new root problem was found.
refresh_events.validator_indexing_effect: No new Wave 1-11 validator routing change; this confirmed stability after adding closeout artifacts.

refresh_events.subject: Refresh discovery indexes for Wave 04A build
refresh_events.captured_head_subject: Tighten build wave routing and validation protocol
refresh_events.why_refresh_happened: The captured head changed top-level build routing, guidance-map routing, script_surface_usage_policy, reference/repo_helpers/index.json, production_authority_index, and scripts/v2_validate_build_wave_protocol.py. Wave 04A build readiness depended on fresh discovery of these rule and validator surfaces.
evidence: Commit 46a5cdb6 updated AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, production_guidance_authority_map, production_authority_index, the durable protocol, and the Wave 02-08 roadmap.
refresh_events.validator_indexing_effect: The build-wave protocol validator became discoverable, active allowlisted, and dispatch allowed for the Wave 04A start gate.

refresh_events.subject: Refresh indexes for validator protocol hardening
refresh_events.why_refresh_happened: The captured head added the explicit targeted_validator_indexing_policy to the durable protocol and roadmap, updated AGENTS.md/SKILL.md, and changed the build-wave validator/tests that enforce the policy. Because these are top-level workflow and discovery rules, a closeout refresh was justified so future agents find the hardened rule through indexes.
evidence: Commit c2ec6296 added the explicit rule that a new wave validator proves its wave when named in the compact slice packet and run directly, without immediate repo/script index refresh.

Commit c2ec6296 added validator enforcement that the durable protocol must mention compact slice packet, focused validation, future discovery, configs/v2/script_surface_usage_policy.json, and reference/repo_helpers/index.json.
refresh_events.validator_indexing_effect: No active allowlist count change; the refresh captured updated validator symbols and protocol references.

active_allowlist_transition.meaning: Wave 1-7 validators indexed as files but not globally routed.
Wave 9 validator indexed as a file but not globally routed.
Wave 10 read-only harness was routed in script_surface_usage_policy.
Wave 11 script_surface_usage_policy expansion routed Wave 1-11 validator surfaces.
Closeout artifact refresh preserved Wave 1-11 routing.
Build-mode validator and related route updates were captured for Wave 04A.
Targeted validator indexing hardening changed symbols/protocol text, not route count.

logic_chain: Waves 1-7 prioritized owner behavior correctness and focused validators/tests. Index freshness was evidence only until Wave 8.
Wave 8 was the first deliberate reconciliation wave, so it refreshed discovery after the foundational surfaces were present.
Wave 9 introduced MCP/runtime precedence, and Wave 10 needed a fresh enough discovery baseline to audit no-write behavior across that new validator.
Wave 10 introduced the reusable read-only harness, and Wave 11 needed that harness indexed and routed for scanner/exact-path cleanup.
Wave 11 globally routed the prior wave validators in script_surface_usage_policy, and the post-Wave-11 closeout refresh made those routes active in the script-import index.
The final closeout refresh indexed the closeout checkpoint/receipt and confirmed stability.
The current Wave 04A/pre-build refreshes happened because top-level protocol, routing, helper, and validator surfaces changed and future discovery depends on them.

current_policy_reinforcement.root_rule: AGENTS.md and SKILL.md now say new wave validators are direct owner-proof surfaces first and should be indexed only when future discovery or another durable refresh condition requires it.
current_policy_reinforcement.enforcer: scripts/v2_validate_build_wave_protocol.py validates that the targeted validator indexing policy exists and names compact slice packet, focused validation, future discovery, script_surface_usage_policy, and repo_helpers boundaries.
current_policy_reinforcement.test: tests/v2/research_factory/test_build_wave_protocol.py rejects missing or weakened targeted validator indexing policy.

conclusion.logic_quality: Coherent. The earlier behavior was not random; it followed a staged pattern of direct focused proof first, discovery reconciliation later when downstream waves needed it.
conclusion.remaining_risk: Low. The risk was that future agents might over-refresh or expect immediate active indexing for every new validator. That risk is now mitigated by root rules, durable protocol, roadmap language, validator enforcement, tests, and fresh indexes.
